steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Translating a graph 8 units down means subtracting 8 from every $y$-value. In $y = mx + b$, a vertical shift changes only the constant term, not the coefficient of $x$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $y = 9x + 8$: adds 8, which shifts the graph up 8 units. ✗
  • Choice B — $y = 9x - 8$: subtracts 8 from the constant term — a vertical shift down 8 units. ✓
  • Choice C — $y = 9(x + 8)$: rewrites as $y = 9x + 72$, a horizontal shift left (and a large vertical offset). ✗
  • Choice D — $y = 9(x - 8)$: rewrites as $y = 9x - 72$, a horizontal shift right. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
